title: Quantum Lab
description: From-scratch quantum lab in TypeScript with FOUR simulation engines: state-vector, density-matrix, stabilizer-tableau (Clifford circuits to 30 qubits), and a Matrix Product State tensor network with a from-scratch complex SVD — running arbitrary circuits on 40 qubits in O(n·χ²) memory with error-tracked bond truncation, perfect sampling and per-cut entanglement entropy. On top of the tensor network: TEBD real-time dynamics of the Ising chain (quench light-cone, matched to exact), and full two-site DMRG — Matrix Product Operators + incremental environments + a matrix-free Lanczos eigensolver finding the ground state of the Ising and Heisenberg/XXZ chains, matching exact diagonalisation to ~1e-13 with a vanishing ⟨H²⟩−⟨H⟩² eigenstate certificate at lengths far past exact diag, plus a phase-transition scan that locates quantum critical points from the ground-state entanglement peak. And now TOPOLOGICAL ERROR CORRECTION: the rotated surface code [[d²,1,d]] with a real Minimum-Weight Perfect Matching decoder — a faithful from-scratch implementation of Edmonds' blossom algorithm (Galil's O(V³) primal-dual matching, verified against brute force) running on a graph of lit-up parity checks weighted by lattice distance. Inject X/Z errors on an interactive lattice and watch the checks fire, the matching form, and the residual be classified as a stabilizer or a logical failure; a Monte-Carlo sweep reproduces the ≈10.3% error-correction threshold where the distance-3/5/7 curves cross. And now FAULT TOLERANCE IN SPACE-TIME: a phenomenological noise model with T repeated noisy syndrome rounds decoded on a 3-D space-time matching graph (time edges absorb measurement errors), a from-scratch Union-Find decoder (Delfosse–Nivelle: near-linear cluster growth + peeling, cross-checked against MWPM), the phenomenological threshold (~3%), and finite-size scaling — the Λ = p_L(d)/p_L(d+2) suppression factor plus a universal data collapse that fits (p_th, ν). And now A GENERAL STABILIZER-CODE ENGINE: a code is just a list of Pauli generators, and the syndrome of an error, the recovery operator, the EXACT code distance (brute-forced as the lightest non-trivial logical over all 4ⁿ Paulis) and whether the code saturates the quantum Hamming bound are all pure GF(2) symplectic linear algebra — no state vector needed. Its headline is the PERFECT FIVE-QUBIT [[5,1,3]] CODE: the smallest code correcting an arbitrary single-qubit error, the first non-CSS (genuinely entangling) code here, whose 16 syndromes are an exact bijection onto the 15 single-qubit errors plus the identity. A new Stabilizer.fromGenerators loads the encoded |0⟩_L straight from the generators (symplectic-dual destabilizer synthesis, no encoding circuit) so a live CHP-tableau cycle — inject, read syndrome, correct, verify — can be cross-checked against the symplectic decoder, and a depolarizing Monte-Carlo sweep exposes each code's pseudo-threshold against the bare-qubit break-even line; the Steane, Shor, [[4,2,2]] detection and 3-qubit repetition codes all live under the same machine-verified engine. Also: Kraus noise, a complex Hermitian eigensolver, Bloch spheres, Schmidt decomposition, error-correcting codes (bit/phase-flip, 9-qubit Shor, Steane [[7,1,3]] with live syndrome decoding), phase estimation, VQE with analytic parameter-shift gradients & QAOA, randomized benchmarking, OpenQASM export, and a 79-case self-test suite cross-checking every engine against exact references.
